question_html:
Correct the dangling modifier.

(a) Walking in the forest, the birds were chirping sweetly.
‎(b) After finishing dinner, the movie was started.
‎(c) Having prepared well, the exam was not so difficult.
‎(d) Looking out of the window, the mountains appeared closer.
‎(e) While baking a cake, the oven door broke.
solution_html:
(a) Walking in the forest, I heard the birds chirping sweetly.
‎(b) After finishing dinner, we started the movie.

Or, When dinner was finished, the movie started.

(c) Having prepared well, I found the exam not so difficult.

Or, Because I had prepared well, the exam seemed easy.
‎(d) Looking out of the window, I thought the mountains appeared closer.

Or, When I looked out of the window, the mountains seemed closer.
‎(e) While baking a cake, I noticed the oven door broke.

Or, As I was baking a cake, the oven door broke.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for writing the correct dangling modifier.

question_html:
Correct the redundancy problems from the following sentences.

(a) They collaborated together on the same idea repeatedly.

(b) I am enclosing herein with my certificates for your reference.

(c) My brother did exactly the same.

(d) In my personal opinion, this project needs more funding.

(e) He returned to the office after lunch.
solution_html:
(a) They collaborated on the idea repeatedly.

(b) I am enclosing my certificates for your reference.

(c) My brother did the same.

(d) In my opinion, this project needs more funding.

(e) He returned to the office after lunch.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for writing the correct redundancy problems from the sentences.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Punctuality’ within 100 words.
solution_html:
Punctuality

Punctuality is the habit of being on time, which reflects a person's discipline and respect for others' time. It is a key component of professionalism and is valued in both personal and professional settings. Being punctual helps build trust and credibility, as it shows reliability and a commitment to meeting obligations. It also enhances productivity by ensuring that tasks start and finish as planned, preventing unnecessary delays. Cultivating punctuality can lead to better time management and create a positive impression, ultimately contributing to personal growth and success in various aspects of life.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing Title, “Punctuality”.

1.5 marks for writing importance of punctuality.

1.5 marks for writing benefits of punctuality.

1.5 marks for writing personal impacts of punctuality.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* Students must write it within 100 words limit. For exceeding the 100 words limit, 0.5 marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
